claim: The reader's reserved belief in the unseen (the protention an unfilled NUL·Pattern holds) should track the recent RATE of newcomers, not accumulated mass. High right after newcomers keep arriving; low after a long stretch of confirmation. A newcomer into a novelty-rich stream is expected and should surprise LESS; into a committed confirmation stream, unexpected, and should surprise MORE.
mechanismTag: novelty-reserve = γ-decayed first-appearance rate (signal-derived amplitude through the fixed Born step), not a 1/(mass+1) constant

mechanismPair.matched: stream mass is matched (both ~1 atom/step), so accumulated mass cannot tell them apart
mechanismPair.underRate: probeSurprise(novel-rich) << probeSurprise(confirm-rich) — the reserve is higher after newcomers, so the newcomer is more expected
mechanismPair.underConst: probeSurprise(novel-rich) == probeSurprise(confirm-rich) — the constant reserve depends only on the (matched) mass, so it is blind to the novelty rate. THIS IS THE GAP.

control.loudSurface: atom diversity AND accumulated mass — recent and stale streams contain the SAME atoms in the SAME counts (matched diversity, ~matched mass); only the TIMING of the newcomers differs (recent vs decayed).
control.underRate: probeSurprise(recent) < probeSurprise(stale) — the rate reserve is higher when the newcomers are RECENT, lower when they have decayed. The win is the γ-recency, exactly the 'recent' in the claim.
control.underConst: probeSurprise(recent) == probeSurprise(stale) — the constant reserve sees the matched mass and is blind to recency. A method that faked the mechanism split by counting distinct atoms (diversity) or mass would give recent==stale and be CAUGHT here: it cannot produce the recency dissociation.

verdictRule: CONFIRMED iff under the rate reserve, in BOTH senses: (a) the mechanism pair dissociates (novel-rich probeSurprise < confirm-rich by a clear margin), AND (b) the control dissociates by recency (recent < stale); AND under the const reserve BOTH collapse (mechanism pair equal AND control equal — the gap is real and not already solved). GAP/FLAT otherwise, per which clause fails.
